cards.name: Healer's Hut
cards.description: Heals all friendly units 3 HP every 8s. Upgrade: heals every 4s.

cards.name: Stone Mason
cards.description: Repairs all friendly walls & structures 8 HP every 10s. Upgrade: every 5s.

cards.name: Blacksmith
cards.description: All friendly units deal +2 damage. Upgrade: +2 more.

cards.name: Fortify
cards.description: Heal all your units for 8 HP.

cards.name: Golem
cards.description: Stone titan — massive HP and damage.

cards.name: Pixie
cards.description: Tiny and flying — bypasses all walls.

cards.name: Rally
cards.description: Heal all your units for 4 HP.

cards.name: Iron Skin
cards.description: All your units gain +15 max HP.

cards.name: Eagle Eye
cards.description: All your units gain +25 attack range.

cards.name: Bloodlust
cards.description: All your units surge with furious speed.

cards.name: Plague Rat
cards.description: Swarm melee. Incredibly fast attack speed.

cards.name: Harpy
cards.description: Flying ranged — swoops over walls and shoots.

cards.name: Ballista
cards.description: Siege bolt — extreme range, fires over walls.

cards.name: Fire Mage
cards.description: Glass cannon — scorches enemies through walls.

cards.name: Executioner
cards.description: Devastating melee blow — one hit, one kill.

cards.name: Mammoth
cards.description: Armored beast — crushes through melee lines.

cards.name: Dark Elf
cards.description: Ranged climber — scales walls then snipes.

cards.name: Giant
cards.description: Towering brute — enormous HP and damage.

cards.name: Wyvern
cards.description: Fast flying ranged — dominates from the air.

cards.name: Field Medic
cards.description: Heal all your units for 6 HP.

cards.name: Titan Blood
cards.description: All your units gain +20 max HP.

cards.name: Sniper Scope
cards.description: All your units gain +30 attack range.

cards.name: Vine Golem
cards.description: Slow forest brute — twisted vines for fists.

cards.name: Thornbeast
cards.description: Climbing beast covered in razor thorns.

cards.name: Thornwall
cards.description: Reinforced thorn barrier — tougher than stone.

cards.name: Ironclad Guard
cards.description: Heavily armored — barely moves, never falls.

cards.name: War Drummer
cards.description: Rallies the march — steady melee brawler.

cards.name: Siege Engineer
cards.description: Ranged expert — fires over walls from distance.

cards.name: Command Tent
cards.description: +1 max mana. The iron citadel runs on logistics.

cards.name: Wight Knight
cards.description: Armored undead — relentless and hard to kill.

cards.name: Ash Elemental
cards.description: Flying scorched spirit — burns through all defenses.

cards.name: Revenant
cards.description: Fast flying ghost — refuses to stay dead.

cards.name: Bone Wall
cards.description: Skeletal barrier — sturdier than it looks.

heroCards.name: Dragon Lord
heroCards.description: HERO: Deploys a mighty Dragon & grants all units +6 attack!

heroCards.name: Battle Queen
heroCards.description: HERO: Deploys an armored Paladin & fortifies all units with +20 max HP!

heroCards.name: Storm Caller
heroCards.description: HERO: Deploys a fearsome Wyvern & blesses all units with +40 attack range!

heroCards.name: Shadow King
heroCards.description: HERO: Deploys a savage Werewolf & surges all units with +25 speed!

heroCards.name: Death Lord
heroCards.description: HERO: Deploys a sinister Lich Apprentice who summons a skeleton army & hastens all units with -500ms attack cooldown!

heroCards.name: Forest Guardian
heroCards.description: HERO: Deploys a mighty Elder Treant & rejuvenates all units with +4 HP heal every 10s!

heroCards.name: Swarm Queen
heroCards.description: HERO: Deploys a mischievous Pixie & commands it to spawn a Spore Bat for you every 10s!

lore.Goblin: Once the cannon fodder of orc warbands, goblins discovered they actually enjoy being first through the door.
lore.Archer: "I don't miss." — Every archer, right before missing.
lore.Barbarian: Trained by no one, feared by everyone. Mostly feared because of the screaming.
lore.Knight: The kingdom's finest. Polished armour hides a spectacular collection of bruises.
lore.Wizard: Years of magical study. Thousands of gold in reagents. Wages still less than a goblin.
lore.Dragon: When it speaks, kingdoms listen. When it breathes, kingdoms burn.
lore.Skeleton: Died once, but keeps showing up. Very dedicated.
lore.Troll: You'll know it's coming. You'll have plenty of time to not do anything about it.
lore.Crossbow: A technically superior weapon. The operators will tell you at every opportunity.
lore.Paladin: Righteous, armoured, and absolutely insufferable at dinner.
lore.Rogue: "What wall?" — The Rogue, from the other side of the wall.
lore.Catapult: Technically pacifist. It's the rocks that do the violence.
lore.Golem: Was told to hold the line. Has been holding it for two hundred years.
lore.Ogre: Large. Loud. Effective. Not necessarily in that order.
lore.Plague Rat: One is an annoyance. A hundred is a catastrophe.
lore.Bandit: Honest work never paid this well.
lore.Bat: Hung upside down until called upon — a skill with surprising tactical applications.
lore.Centaur: The upper half writes poetry. The lower half doesn't know.
lore.Harpy: Sings beautifully. Fights savagely. Never at the same time.
lore.Specter: Died defending the post. Still defending it.
lore.Ballista: "Fire the ballista!" — said with far too much enthusiasm every time.
lore.Vampire: Charming until the biting starts. Sometimes also during the biting.
lore.Griffin: Half eagle, half lion, all opinion.
lore.Executioner: Does not like being told there is more than one swing allowed.
lore.Mammoth: Once a peaceful grazer. Someone put armour on it.
lore.Dark Elf: Watches from the shadows. Shoots from the shadows. Lives in the shadows. Hates dentist appointments.
lore.Necromancer: Death is just unemployment with extra steps.
lore.Giant: Slow. Inevitable. Cannot open doors, but rarely needs to.
lore.Wyvern: The dragon's practical cousin. Less fire, more punctuality.
lore.Behemoth: There is no plan B for a Behemoth. There is often no plan A left either.
lore.Vine Golem: The forest did not build it. The forest became it.
lore.Spore Bat: Releases a cloud of confusion before the claws arrive.
lore.Thornbeast: Whatever it used to be, the forest has claimed it fully.
lore.Elder Treant: It has been here since before the Fracture. It plans to be here after.
lore.Frog Knight: Somehow obtained full plate armour. Nobody asked where.
lore.Ironclad Guard: Has never retreated. Has never needed to.
lore.War Drummer: The beat sets the pace. The pace sets the outcome.
lore.Siege Engineer: Turns distance into an advantage. Turns walls into a suggestion.
lore.Shield Wall Soldier: The first to arrive. The last to fall. Usually the first to fall.
lore.Grizzled Veteran: Has survived things that should not be survivable. Very annoyed about it.
lore.Bone Archer: Accuracy improves significantly without the burden of a heartbeat.
lore.Wight Knight: The armour corrodes. The knight does not.
lore.Ash Elemental: Born from the Fracture's scorched aftermath. Still burning.
lore.Revenant: Kept returning until everyone stopped asking why.
lore.Lich Apprentice: Still learning. Still deadly. The tuition is paid in souls.
lore.Stone Wall: The first answer to every problem. Usually the second answer too.
lore.Farm: Food is mana. Mana is power. This farmer has no idea what he's contributing to.
lore.Mana Spring: Nobody can explain where the water comes from. Nobody is asking anymore.
lore.Healer's Hut: "Rest," she says. "It'll hurt less tomorrow." She is usually right.
lore.Stone Mason: Builds walls by day, repairs them by night. He's given up asking why they keep breaking.
lore.Blacksmith: Hammer, anvil, fire. In that order. He has opinions about everything else.
lore.Barracks: Turns gold into goblins. Economists are still debating whether that's profitable.
lore.Arcane Tower: The archers it trains insist they learned everything naturally.
lore.Dragon Lair: Do not approach. Do not inquire. Absolutely do not ask about the rent.
lore.Crypt: Nobody asked what's in there. Everyone agrees not to.
lore.Troll Den: Smells terrible. Keeps the neighbours away.
lore.Garrison: A crossbow and a grudge, supplied on rotation.
lore.Cathedral: Blessed walls. Blessed warriors. Blessed inconvenient for the enemy.
lore.Rogue Den: No sign. No address. Impossible to miss.
lore.Siege Works: The engineers here have never met a wall they didn't want to destroy.
lore.Dark Shrine: Full moon schedule posted on the door.
lore.Iron Forge: Hammers day and night. Nobody sleeps within a mile.
lore.Fairy Ring: "Step inside the ring," they said. "It'll be fun," they said.
lore.Ogre Den: Listed as "residential" in the tax records.
lore.Rat Burrow: The smell announces them long before they arrive.
lore.Bat Cave: Dark inside. Dark outside. Mostly just dark.
lore.Scorpion Pit: HR has filed fourteen complaints about working conditions.
lore.Centaur Stable: It's not a stable, they keep insisting.
lore.Spirit Well: The water is fine. The spirits are not.
lore.Ballista Tower: The elevator is broken. Engineers don't care.
lore.Vamp. Coven: Meets at midnight. Dress code: dramatic.
lore.Aerie: Spectacular view. Treacherous access. Worth it.
lore.Mage Tower: Full of books, experiments, and extremely anxious junior mages.
lore.Mammoth Pen: Do not feed after dark. Do not feed before dark either.
lore.Shadow Academy: Curriculum: archery, shadows, and sighing at lesser beings.
lore.Death Tower: The door is unlocked. Nobody goes in uninvited.
lore.Wyvern Roost: Perched atop the cliff where nothing else dares to live.
lore.Ancient Altar: Old beyond memory. Hungry beyond measure.
lore.Thornwall: The thorns grow back faster than you can cut them.
lore.Ancient Grove: Old enough to remember when the forest covered everything.
lore.Armory: Every soldier who leaves here is better armed. Most come back.
lore.Command Tent: Where strategy happens. Mostly arguing, but strategy.
lore.Bone Wall: Unpleasant to look at. Effective to hide behind.
lore.Soul Obelisk: Whispers things. Nobody agrees on what.
lore.Sharpen Blades: The whetstone whine echoes across camp. Everyone quietly checks their grip.
lore.Rally: A rousing speech, a quick bandage — good as new.
lore.Haste: Legs moved so fast they filed a formal complaint.
lore.Eagle Eye: See further. Regret it only slightly more often.
lore.Field Medic: Not a real medic. Effective anyway.
lore.Sniper Scope: For those who believe range is the solution to all problems.
lore.Dragon Lord: Commands dragons. Dragons pretend not to notice.
lore.Battle Queen: Won sixteen wars. Lost zero negotiations.
lore.Storm Caller: The sky does what she says. Eventually.
lore.Shadow King: Feared in twelve kingdoms. Banned from two taverns specifically.
lore.Death Lord: Death is just a job. A very grim job, but somebody's got to do it.
lore.Forest Guardian: The forest is alive. It has opinions on your strategy.
lore.Swarm Queen: The swarm is a family. A very bitey family.
lore.Graham The All Seeing: Sees all. Knows all. Still can't find his keys. Play tested by Graham, who is indeed all seeing.
